exercises.name: Dumbbell Renegade Row to Push Up
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, from a plank, row each weight to your ribs (renegade row), then perform a push‑up.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Renegade Row to Push-Up
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position with a dumbbell in each hand, directly under your shoulders, feet slightly wider than hip-width for stability. 

Alternate rows by pulling one dumbbell toward your ribcage, then the other, keeping your core tight and hips stable. 

After completing one row with each hand, perform a push-up by bending both elbows and lowering your chest toward the floor. 

Press back up to the plank position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1) Stand with dumbbells in both hands hanging down your sides. 

2) Extend one leg back and lower your body on the other leg until knee of rear leg is almost in contact with floor. 

3) Return to original standing position. 

4) Make sure your torso is straight during the whole exercise.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge into Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step back into a reverse lunge, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge to Press
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shoulders, step back into a reverse lunge. After standing up, press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge to Shoulder Press
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Reverse Single leg Lunge to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step back into a reverse lunge, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge with Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step back into a reverse lunge, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian (RDL) Deadlift |TECHNIQUE for Beginners
exercises.description: Dumbbell Romanian (RDL) Deadlift |TECHNIQUE for Beginners

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description:
Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding dumbbells in front of your thighs. 

Hinge at your hips, lowering the weights down your legs while keeping your back straight. 

Return to standing by driving your hips forward.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Arnold Press
exercises.description:
1) Sit on an exercise bench with back support and hold two dumbbells in front of you at about upper chest level with your palms facing your body and your elbows bent. 

2) Raise the dumbbells as you rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward. 

3) Continue lifting the dumbbells until your arms are extended above you in straight arm position. 

4) Breathe out as you perform this portion of the move

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Back Fly
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, keep your body and your back in a straight or slightly arched position 

2) Have the dumbbells in each hand. 

3) Move the weights out and away from each other in an arc motion. 

4) The arms should be elevated until they are parallel to the floor. 

5) Slowly lower the weights back down 

6) Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Front Raise
exercises.alternateName: Front delts shoudler raise incline bench strict
exercises.description:
1) Lay back on an incline bench with the incline set anywhere between 30 to 60 degrees. 

2) Hold two dumbbells just above the knees with your arms straight and your palms facing down. 

3) Raise the dumbbells up until they are slightly above your shoulders, pause, and then lower them to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, keep your elbows pointing straight up to the ceiling and in line with one another. 

2) Arm should be parallel and in line with your ear when you extend over top of your head as well as in the downward position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Start with both dumbbells positioned to each side of the shoulders with at a 90 degree fashion. Press dumbbells upwards until arms are extended overhead. Lower and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Tricep Kickbacks
exercises.description:
1) Sit on the edge of a bench and hold dumbbell down to your side palm facing in. 

2) Pull your upper arm as high as you can while keeping your forearm vertical to the ground. 

3) Start by pressing dumbbell back in a semicircular motion until the entire arm is stretched out and parallel to the floor. 

4) Pause, and return to start position. Make sure your upper arm is still at all times.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Tricep Press
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright on a bench, dumbbell held with both hands behind your head, palms facing up.

2.Raise your arms straight above your head, holding the weight in place.

3.With your elbows still, lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your triceps. 

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Scaptions
exercises.description:
Stand with dumbbells at your sides. 

Raise arms forward at a 45-degree angle, thumbs pointing up, to shoulder height. 

Lower back down slowly. Focus on controlled movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.description:
1) Stand and hold two dumbbells with your arms hanging at your sides. 

2) Begin by raising your shoulders up as far as possible, pause, and then return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Side Bend
exercises.description:
1) Stand straight up with a dumbbell in your right hand and your back straight. 

2) Place your left hand on your waist. Begin by bending to the right as far as you can, and then to the left as far as you can, using only your waist. 

3) After completing your target number of reps, switch the dumbbell to the other hand and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Side Lunge to High Pull
exercises.description:
Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand opposite to the leg you will step out. 

Step out laterally into a side lunge, bending the stepping knee while keeping the opposite leg straight and chest upright. As you lunge, lower the dumbbell toward the stepping leg. 

Push through the stepping foot to return to standing, then perform a high pull with the same hand by pulling the dumbbell toward your chin, elbow leading and slightly flared. 

Lower the dumbbell back to your side and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Side Plank Reverse Fly
exercises.description:
Get into a side plank position with a dumbbell in the top hand. 

Extend the dumbbell toward the floor, then lift it to shoulder height in a reverse fly motion. Keep hips lifted.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding one dumbbell with your arm straight and your palm facing out.

2.Raise the dumbbell up to shoulder height, bending at the elbow. Do not turn at the wrist, finish with your palm facing back.

3.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Clean
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shin, the other hand for counter balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbells straight up, catch them at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Clean and Jerk
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand between your legs at your shins with your arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward pulling the dumbbell straight up, catching it at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Quickly press the dumbbell overhead splitting your feet front to back to absorb it at the top.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Clean and Press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shins with your arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbell straight up, catch it at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Quickly push the dumbbell overhead, extending your arms and legs fully.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Floor Press
exercises.description:
1.Lie on the floor with one hand holding the dumbbell, at shoulder level, palm facing up. The other hand placed on the floor for stability.

2.Push the dumbbell straight up toward the ceiling.

3.Lower the dumbbell returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Hammer Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, one hand holding the dumbbell, palm facing the body and arm resting on the pad.

2.Curl the dumbbell up to shoulder height, bending at the elbows. Keep your shoulder steady as your raise the dumbbell.

3.Lower the dumbbell returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm High Pull
exercises.description:
1.Hold one dumbbell at one knee in a slight squat with your arm straight.

2.Extend your hips and rapidly raise the dumbbell up to just below your chin, keeping your elbow above your hand and the dumbbell close to your body.

3.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Kickstand Deadlift
exercises.description: Start in a kickstand position and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite to the forward leg. Hinge to lower the weight and stand tall.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.alternateName: Single Arm Lateral Deltoid side Fly
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, set your feet close together with a soft knee bend. 

2. Hold a dumbbell using one of your hands, the palm of the hand facing you. Put your non-lifting hand on your hip. Keep your chest upright. This will be your starting position.

3. Lift the dumbbell to your side until your arm is parallel to the floor. Exhale as you execute this movement and pause for a second at the top.

4. Lower the dumbbell back down slowly to the starting position as you inhale.

5. Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

6. Once you're done one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1) Hold a dumbbell right behind your neck with your elbow pointing upward. 

2) Use your tricep to left the weight and extend your arm until straight. 

3) Slowly return back to start and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Plank Pull to Press
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand directly beneath your shoulders. 

Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels and your core engaged. 

Row one dumbbell toward your ribcage while stabilizing with the other arm, then press that dumbbell overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1) Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, one hand holding the dumbbell, palm facing up and arm resting on the pad.

2) Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder. Pause.

3) Lower the dumbbell returning to starting position.

4) Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
1) Place one knee on a bench, one hand in a push-up position. Body parallel to the floor with back straight, one hand holding the dumbbell onto your side.

2) Pull the dumbbell up to the side of your chest, keeping arm close to the body.

3) Lower the dumbbell returning to starting position.

4) Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a dumbbell on one hand at shoulder height, fist away from you.

2.Press the dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.

3.Keep your back flat and remain upright throughout.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Snatch
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shin with your arm straight, the other hand for counter balance.

2.Maintaining squat position, raise the dumbbell up, arm extended straight overhead.

3.Keeping the arm extended overhead, stand upright.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Snatch to Overhead Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding one dumbbell between your legs with an overhand grip. Hinge your hips back and lower the dumbbell toward the floor. 

In one motion, drive through your hips and clean the dumbbell up to your shoulder as you step one leg back into a reverse lunge. 

From there, press the dumbbell overhead while keeping your core tight. Push through your front heel to return to standing, then lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Squat Clean
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shins, arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbell straight up, catch it at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees.

3.Maintaining the dumbbell at shoulder height, stand upright, legs extended.

4.Return to starting position. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Squat Clean and Jerk
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shins, arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.ln one motion rapidly raise the dumbbell to your shoulder level.

3.Rapidly extend your hips and knees, pulling the dumbbell up, keeping your arm straight and shrugging your shoulders. Drop down as the dumbbell travel up and absorb them in a low squat

4.Stand upright with the dumbbell at shoulder height. Then return to starting position. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Squat Clean and Press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shins, arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbell straight up, catch it at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees.

3.Quickly push the dumbbell overhead, extending your arms and legs fully.

4.Bring the dumbbell at shoulder height, then return to starting position. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.
